# 基于 Spring AI 开发的 AI 项目目录结构

目前业界基于 Spring AI 的项目主要分为两种层次：**框架本身的模块结构** 和 **应用级项目的目录结构**。下面分别介绍。

---

## 一、应用级项目目录结构（最常见）

这是企业中基于 Spring AI 开发 RAG/Chatbot/Agent 应用时最典型的目录结构：

```
my-ai-app/
├── pom.xml (或 build.gradle)
├── src/
│   ├── main/
│   │   ├── java/com/yourcompany/aiapp/
│   │   │   ├── config/                    # 配置层
│   │   │   │   ├── AiConfig.java          # Spring AI 模型配置（ChatClient、EmbeddingModel）
│   │   │   │   ├── VectorStoreConfig.java # 向量数据库配置（PGVector/Milvus等）
│   │   │   │   ├── SecurityConfig.java    # 安全配置（JWT等）
│   │   │   │   └── RateLimitConfig.java   # 限流配置
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── controller/                # 控制器层
│   │   │   │   ├── ChatController.java    # 聊天接口（支持SSE流式输出）
│   │   │   │   ├── DocumentController.java# 文档上传/管理接口
│   │   │   │   └── AgentController.java   # Agent 工作流接口
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── service/                   # 业务逻辑层
│   │   │   │   ├── ChatService.java       # 聊天服务，调用 ChatClient
│   │   │   │   ├── RagService.java        # RAG 编排（检索+增强+生成）
│   │   │   │   ├── EmbeddingService.java  # 向量嵌入生成
│   │   │   │   ├── DocumentService.java   # 文档解析与处理
│   │   │   │   ├── IngestionService.java  # ETL 管道（分块、向量化、入库）
│   │   │   │   └── AgentService.java      # Agent/工具调用编排
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── advisor/                   # Spring AI Advisor 层
│   │   │   │   ├── MemoryAdvisor.java     # 对话记忆增强
│   │   │   │   ├── RagAdvisor.java        # RAG 上下文增强
│   │   │   │   └── LoggingAdvisor.java    # 日志/可观测性
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── tool/                      # Function Calling / 工具定义
│   │   │   │   ├── WeatherTool.java       # 天气查询工具
│   │   │   │   ├── DatabaseTool.java      # 数据库查询工具
│   │   │   │   └── SearchTool.java        # 搜索工具
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── model/                     # 数据模型层
│   │   │   │   ├── dto/                   # 请求/响应 DTO
│   │   │   │   │   ├── ChatRequest.java
│   │   │   │   │   └── ChatResponse.java
│   │   │   │   └── entity/               # 数据库实体
│   │   │   │       ├── Conversation.java
│   │   │   │       └── Document.java
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── repository/               # 数据访问层
│   │   │   │   ├── ConversationRepository.java
│   │   │   │   └── DocumentRepository.java
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   └── AiApplication.java        # 启动类
│   │   │
│   │   └── resources/
│   │       ├── application.yml            # 主配置（API Key、模型参数等）
│   │       ├── application-dev.yml        # 开发环境配置
│   │       ├── application-prod.yml       # 生产环境配置
│   │       ├── prompts/                   # Prompt 模板目录
│   │       │   ├── system-prompt.st       # 系统提示词模板
│   │       │   ├── rag-prompt.st          # RAG 提示词模板
│   │       │   └── agent-prompt.st        # Agent 提示词模板
│   │       └── documents/                 # 知识库文档（开发用）
│   │
│   └── test/
│       └── java/com/yourcompany/aiapp/
│           ├── service/
│           └── controller/
│
├── docker-compose.yml                     # PostgreSQL+pgvector / Redis 等
└── README.md
```

---

## 二、关键分层说明

| 层级 | 职责 | Spring AI 关键组件 |
|------|------|--------------------|
| **Config** | 配置 ChatClient、EmbeddingModel、VectorStore | `ChatClient.Builder`、`@ConfigurationProperties` |
| **Controller** | REST/SSE 接口，接收用户请求 | `Flux<String>` 流式响应 |
| **Service** | 核心业务逻辑，RAG 编排，Agent 调度 | `ChatClient`、`VectorStore`、`DocumentReader` |
| **Advisor** | 请求/响应的拦截与增强（类似 AOP） | `MessageChatMemoryAdvisor`、`QuestionAnswerAdvisor` |
| **Tool** | Function Calling 工具定义 | `@Tool` 注解、`ToolCallback` |
| **Model/DTO** | 数据传输对象 | — |
| **Repository** | 数据持久化 | Spring Data JPA |

---

## 三、Spring AI 框架本身的模块结构

如果你对框架源码级结构感兴趣，Spring AI 2.x 采用如下多模块设计：

```
spring-ai/
├── spring-ai-commons/              # 基础公共模块（JSON、可观测性）
├── spring-ai-model/                # AI 能力抽象（ChatModel、EmbeddingModel、ImageModel）
├── spring-ai-client-chat/          # 高级对话 API（ChatClient）
├── spring-ai-vector-store/         # 向量数据库抽象
├── spring-ai-rag/                  # RAG 框架
├── models/                         # 20+ 模型实现
│   ├── spring-ai-openai/
│   ├── spring-ai-anthropic/
│   ├── spring-ai-ollama/
│   ├── spring-ai-qwen/             # 通义千问
│   └── ...
├── vector-stores/                  # 20+ 向量存储实现
│   ├── spring-ai-pgvector-store/
│   ├── spring-ai-milvus-store/
│   ├── spring-ai-qdrant-store/
│   └── ...
├── document-readers/               # 文档读取器
│   ├── spring-ai-pdf-document-reader/
│   ├── spring-ai-tika-document-reader/
│   └── ...
├── memory/                         # 对话记忆存储
│   ├── spring-ai-memory-repository-jdbc/
│   └── spring-ai-memory-repository-mongodb/
├── spring-ai-spring-boot-autoconfigure/   # 自动配置
├── spring-ai-spring-boot-starters/        # Starter 依赖
└── spring-ai-bom/                         # 版本管理 BOM
```

---

## 四、当前业界技术栈搭配

| 组件 | 主流选择 |
|------|---------|
| **JDK** | 17+（推荐 21，支持虚拟线程） |
| **Spring Boot** | 3.4+（Spring AI 1.x）或 4.x（Spring AI 2.x） |
| **LLM 提供商** | OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama（本地） / 通义千问 |
| **向量数据库** | PostgreSQL + pgvector（最主流，可与业务数据共库）或 Milvus |
| **对话记忆** | JDBC / Redis |
| **文档解析** | Tika（通用）、PDF Reader |
| **部署** | Docker Compose / Kubernetes |
| **可观测性** | Micrometer + Prometheus + Grafana |

---

## 五、核心设计理念

1. **抽象即自由** — 通过 `ChatClient` 和 `ChatModel` 屏蔽不同 LLM 厂商差异，一次编码可切换多个模型
2. **Advisor 模式** — 类似 Spring MVC 的拦截器，可以链式增强对话（加记忆、加 RAG 上下文、加日志）
3. **与 Spring 生态无缝集成** — 天然支持 Spring Security、Actuator、Spring Data、Spring Batch 等
4. **Prompt 模板化** — 将提示词放在 `resources/prompts/` 下用模板引擎管理，支持变量替换

这种结构已经是当前 Java/Spring 生态做 AI 应用的事实标准，适用于智能客服、文档问答、Agent 工作流等场景。

```json
{
  "markdown": "Getting Started :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Getting Started\n\nThis section offers jumping off points for how to get started using Spring AI.\n\nYou should follow the steps in each of the following sections according to your needs.\n\n| Spring AI supports Spring Boot 3.4.x and 3.5.x. |\n| --- |\n\n## Spring Initializr\n\nHead on over to [start.spring.io](https://start.spring.io/) and select the AI Models and Vector Stores that you want to use in your new applications.\n\n## Artifact Repositories\n\n### Releases - Use Maven Central\n\nSpring AI 1.0.0 and later versions are available in Maven Central. No additional repository configuration is required. Just make sure you have Maven Central enabled in your build file.\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!-- Maven Central is included by default in Maven builds.\n     You usually don’t need to configure it explicitly,\n     but it's shown here for clarity. --\u003e\n\u003crepositories\u003e\n    \u003crepository\u003e\n        \u003cid\u003ecentral\u003c/id\u003e\n        \u003curl\u003ehttps://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2\u003c/url\u003e\n    \u003c/repository\u003e\n\u003c/repositories\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrepositories {\n    mavenCentral()\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Snapshots - Add Snapshot Repositories\n\nTo use the latest development versions (e.g.&#96;1.1.0-SNAPSHOT&#96;) or older milestone versions before 1.0.0, you need to add the following snapshot repositories in your build file.\n\nAdd the following repository definitions to your Maven or Gradle build file:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003crepositories\u003e\n  \u003crepository\u003e\n    \u003cid\u003espring-snapshots\u003c/id\u003e\n    \u003cname\u003eSpring Snapshots\u003c/name\u003e\n    \u003curl\u003ehttps://repo.spring.io/snapshot\u003c/url\u003e\n    \u003creleases\u003e\n      \u003cenabled\u003efalse\u003c/enabled\u003e\n    \u003c/releases\u003e\n  \u003c/repository\u003e\n  \u003crepository\u003e\n    \u003cname\u003eCentral Portal Snapshots\u003c/name\u003e\n    \u003cid\u003ecentral-portal-snapshots\u003c/id\u003e\n    \u003curl\u003ehttps://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/\u003c/url\u003e\n    \u003creleases\u003e\n      \u003cenabled\u003efalse\u003c/enabled\u003e\n    \u003c/releases\u003e\n    \u003csnapshots\u003e\n      \u003cenabled\u003etrue\u003c/enabled\u003e\n    \u003c/snapshots\u003e\n  \u003c/repository\u003e\n\u003c/repositories\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n  maven { url 'https://repo.spring.io/milestone' }\n  maven { url 'https://repo.spring.io/snapshot' }\n  maven {\n    name = 'Central Portal Snapshots'\n    url = 'https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/'\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNOTE: When using Maven with Spring AI snapshots, pay attention to your Maven mirror configuration. If you have configured a mirror in your&#96;settings.xml&#96; like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cmirror\u003e\n    \u003cid\u003emy-mirror\u003c/id\u003e\n    \u003cmirrorOf\u003e*\u003c/mirrorOf\u003e\n    \u003curl\u003ehttps://my-company-repository.com/maven\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003c/mirror\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe wildcard&#96;*&#96; will redirect all repository requests to your mirror, preventing access to Spring snapshot repositories. To fix this, modify the&#96;mirrorOf&#96; configuration to exclude Spring repositories:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cmirror\u003e\n    \u003cid\u003emy-mirror\u003c/id\u003e\n    \u003cmirrorOf\u003e*,!spring-snapshots,!central-portal-snapshots\u003c/mirrorOf\u003e\n    \u003curl\u003ehttps://my-company-repository.com/maven\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003c/mirror\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis configuration allows Maven to access Spring snapshot repositories directly while still using your mirror for other dependencies.\n\n## Dependency Management\n\nThe Spring AI Bill of Materials (BOM) declares the recommended versions of all the dependencies used by a given release of Spring AI. This is a BOM-only version and it just contains dependency management and no plugin declarations or direct references to Spring or Spring Boot. You can use the Spring Boot parent POM, or use the BOM from Spring Boot (&#96;spring-boot-dependencies&#96;) to manage Spring Boot versions.\n\nAdd the BOM to your project:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependencyManagement\u003e\n    \u003cdependencies\u003e\n        \u003cdependency\u003e\n            \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n            \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-bom\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n            \u003cversion\u003e1.0.0\u003c/version\u003e\n            \u003ctype\u003epom\u003c/type\u003e\n            \u003cscope\u003eimport\u003c/scope\u003e\n        \u003c/dependency\u003e\n    \u003c/dependencies\u003e\n\u003c/dependencyManagement\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n  implementation platform(\"org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-bom:1.0.0\")\n  // Replace the following with the specific module dependencies (e.g., spring-ai-openai) or starter modules (e.g., spring-ai-starter-model-openai) that you wish to use\n  implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-openai'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nGradle users can also use the Spring AI BOM by leveraging Gradle (5.0+) native support for declaring dependency constraints using a Maven BOM. This is implemented by adding a 'platform' dependency handler method to the dependencies section of your Gradle build script.\n\n## Add dependencies for specific components\n\nEach of the following sections in the documentation shows which dependencies you need to add to your project build system.\n\n[Chat Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html)\n\n[Embeddings Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings.html)\n\n[Image Generation Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/imageclient.html)\n\n[Transcription Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/audio/transcriptions.html)\n\n[Text-To-Speech (TTS) Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/audio/speech.html)\n\n[Vector Databases](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs.html)\n\n## Spring AI samples\n\nPlease refer to [this page](https://github.com/spring-ai-community/awesome-spring-ai) for more resources and samples related to Spring AI.",
```

```json
{
  "markdown": "Chat Model API :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Chat Model API\n\nThe Chat Model API offers developers the ability to integrate AI-powered chat completion capabilities into their applications. It leverages pre-trained language models, such as GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer), to generate human-like responses to user inputs in natural language.\n\nThe API typically works by sending a prompt or partial conversation to the AI model, which then generates a completion or continuation of the conversation based on its training data and understanding of natural language patterns. The completed response is then returned to the application, which can present it to the user or use it for further processing.\n\nThe&#96;Spring AI Chat Model API&#96; is designed to be a simple and portable interface for interacting with various [AI Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/concepts.html#_models), allowing developers to switch between different models with minimal code changes. This design aligns with Spring’s philosophy of modularity and interchangeability.\n\nAlso with the help of companion classes like&#96;Prompt&#96; for input encapsulation and&#96;ChatResponse&#96; for output handling, the Chat Model API unifies the communication with AI Models. It manages the complexity of request preparation and response parsing, offering a direct and simplified API interaction.\n\nYou can find more about available implementations in the Available Implementations section as well as detailed comparison in the [Chat Models Comparison](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/comparison.html) section.\n\n## API Overview\n\nThis section provides a guide to the Spring AI Chat Model API interface and associated classes.\n\n### ChatModel\n\nHere is the [ChatModel](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/ChatModel.java) interface definition:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface ChatModel extends Model\u003cPrompt, ChatResponse\u003e, StreamingChatModel {\n\n\tdefault String call(String message) {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tChatResponse call(Prompt prompt);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;call()&#96; method with a&#96;String&#96; parameter simplifies initial use, avoiding the complexities of the more sophisticated&#96;Prompt&#96; and&#96;ChatResponse&#96; classes. In real-world applications, it is more common to use the&#96;call()&#96; method that takes a&#96;Prompt&#96; instance and returns a&#96;ChatResponse&#96;.\n\n### StreamingChatModel\n\nHere is the [StreamingChatModel](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/StreamingChatModel.java) interface definition:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface StreamingChatModel extends StreamingModel\u003cPrompt, ChatResponse\u003e {\n\n    default Flux\u003cString\u003e stream(String message) {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tFlux\u003cChatResponse\u003e stream(Prompt prompt);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;stream()&#96; method takes a&#96;String&#96; or&#96;Prompt&#96; parameter similar to&#96;ChatModel&#96; but it streams the responses using the reactive Flux API.\n\n### Prompt\n\nThe [Prompt](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-client-chat/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/prompt/Prompt.java) is a&#96;ModelRequest&#96; that encapsulates a list of [Message](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/messages/Message.java) objects and optional model request options. The following listing shows a truncated version of the&#96;Prompt&#96; class, excluding constructors and other utility methods:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class Prompt implements ModelRequest\u003cList\u003cMessage\u003e\u003e {\n\n    private final List\u003cMessage\u003e messages;\n\n    private ChatOptions modelOptions;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatOptions getOptions() {...}\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic List\u003cMessage\u003e getInstructions() {...}\n\n    // constructors and utility methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Message\n\nThe&#96;Message&#96; interface encapsulates a&#96;Prompt&#96; textual content, a collection of metadata attributes, and a categorization known as&#96;MessageType&#96;.\n\nThe interface is defined as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface Content {\n\n\tString getText();\n\n\tMap\u003cString, Object\u003e getMetadata();\n}\n\npublic interface Message extends Content {\n\n\tMessageType getMessageType();\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe multimodal message types implement also the&#96;MediaContent&#96; interface providing a list of&#96;Media&#96; content objects.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface MediaContent extends Content {\n\n\tCollection\u003cMedia\u003e getMedia();\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;Message&#96; interface has various implementations that correspond to the categories of messages that an AI model can process:\n\nThe chat completion endpoint, distinguish between message categories based on conversational roles, effectively mapped by the&#96;MessageType&#96;.\n\nFor instance, OpenAI recognizes message categories for distinct conversational roles such as&#96;system&#96;,&#96;user&#96;,&#96;function&#96;, or&#96;assistant&#96;.\n\nWhile the term&#96;MessageType&#96; might imply a specific message format, in this context it effectively designates the role a message plays in the dialogue.\n\nFor AI models that do not use specific roles, the&#96;UserMessage&#96; implementation acts as a standard category, typically representing user-generated inquiries or instructions. To understand the practical application and the relationship between&#96;Prompt&#96; and&#96;Message&#96;, especially in the context of these roles or message categories, see the detailed explanations in the [Prompts](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/prompt.html) section.\n\n#### Chat Options\n\nRepresents the options that can be passed to the AI model. The&#96;ChatOptions&#96; class is a subclass of&#96;ModelOptions&#96; and is used to define few portable options that can be passed to the AI model. The&#96;ChatOptions&#96; class is defined as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface ChatOptions extends ModelOptions {\n\n\tString getModel();\n\tFloat getFrequencyPenalty();\n\tInteger getMaxTokens();\n\tFloat getPresencePenalty();\n\tList\u003cString\u003e getStopSequences();\n\tFloat getTemperature();\n\tInteger getTopK();\n\tFloat getTopP();\n\tChatOptions copy();\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAdditionally, every model specific ChatModel/StreamingChatModel implementation can have its own options that can be passed to the AI model. For example, the OpenAI Chat Completion model has its own options like&#96;logitBias&#96;,&#96;seed&#96;, and&#96;user&#96;.\n\nThis is a powerful feature that allows developers to use model-specific options when starting the application and then override them at runtime using the&#96;Prompt&#96; request.\n\nSpring AI provides a sophisticated system for configuring and using Chat Models. It allows for default configurations to be set at start-up, while also providing the flexibility to override these settings on a per-request basis. This approach enables developers to easily work with different AI models and adjust parameters as needed, all within a consistent interface provided by the Spring AI framework.\n\nFollowing flow diagram illustrates how Spring AI handles the configuration and execution of Chat Models, combining start-up and runtime options:\n\nStart-up Configuration - The ChatModel/StreamingChatModel is initialized with \"Start-Up\" Chat Options. These options are set during the ChatModel initialization and are meant to provide default configurations.\n\nRuntime Configuration - For each request, the Prompt can contain a Runtime Chat Options: These can override the start-up options.\n\nOption Merging Process - The \"Merge Options\" step combines the start-up and runtime options. If runtime options are provided, they take precedence over the start-up options.\n\nInput Processing - The \"Convert Input\" step transforms the input instructions into native, model-specific formats.\n\nOutput Processing - The \"Convert Output\" step transforms the model’s response into a standardized&#96;ChatResponse&#96; format.\n\nThe separation of start-up and runtime options allows for both global configurations and request-specific adjustments.\n\n### ChatResponse\n\nThe structure of the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; class is as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class ChatResponse implements ModelResponse\u003cGeneration\u003e {\n\n    private final ChatResponseMetadata chatResponseMetadata;\n\tprivate final List\u003cGeneration\u003e generations;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatResponseMetadata getMetadata() {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tpublic List\u003cGeneration\u003e getResults() {...}\n\n    // other methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe [ChatResponse](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/ChatResponse.java) class holds the AI Model’s output, with each&#96;Generation&#96; instance containing one of potentially multiple outputs resulting from a single prompt.\n\nThe&#96;ChatResponse&#96; class also carries a&#96;ChatResponseMetadata&#96; metadata about the AI Model’s response.\n\n### Generation\n\nFinally, the [Generation](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/Generation.java) class extends from the&#96;ModelResult&#96; to represent the model output (assistant message) and related metadata:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class Generation implements ModelResult\u003cAssistantMessage\u003e {\n\n\tprivate final AssistantMessage assistantMessage;\n\tprivate ChatGenerationMetadata chatGenerationMetadata;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic AssistantMessage getOutput() {...}\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatGenerationMetadata getMetadata() {...}\n\n    // other methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Available Implementations\n\nThis diagram illustrates the unified interfaces,&#96;ChatModel&#96; and&#96;StreamingChatModel&#96;, are used for interacting with various AI chat models from different providers, allowing easy integration and switching between different AI services while maintaining a consistent API for the client application.\n\n[OpenAI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n[Microsoft Azure Open AI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/azure-openai-chat.html)(streaming \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n[Ollama Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/ollama-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n[Hugging Face Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/huggingface.html)(no streaming support)\n\n[Google Vertex AI Gemini Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/vertexai-gemini-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n[Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/bedrock.html)\n\n[Mistral AI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/mistralai-chat.html)(streaming \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n[Anthropic Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/anthropic-chat.html)(streaming \u0026 function-calling support)\n\n| Find a detailed comparison of the available Chat Models in the [Chat Models Comparison](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/comparison.html) section. |\n| --- |\n\n## Chat Model API\n\nThe Spring AI Chat Model API is built on top of the Spring AI&#96;Generic Model API&#96; providing Chat specific abstractions and implementations. This allows an easy integration and switching between different AI services while maintaining a consistent API for the client application. The following class diagram illustrates the main classes and interfaces of the Spring AI Chat Model API.",
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html"
}
```

已完成所有项目级别 Rules 的创建。以下是生成的 4 个规则文件概览：

---

### 规则文件清单

| 文件 | 作用域 | 说明 |
|------|--------|------|
| `spring-ai-project.mdc` | **始终生效** | 项目架构总览：技术栈版本、目录结构、分层架构、Maven BOM 配置 |
| `java-conventions.mdc` | `**/*.java` | Java 21 编码规范：Record/虚拟线程等新特性、命名规范、异常处理、依赖注入 |
| `spring-ai-patterns.mdc` | `**/*.java` | Spring AI 开发模式：ChatClient 用法、Advisor 链、Tool Calling、RAG 流程、流式响应 |
| `config-conventions.mdc` | `resources/**/*.{yml,yaml,properties,st}` | 配置文件规范：多环境管理、敏感信息注入、核心配置项模板、Prompt 模板管理 |

### 版本说明

- **Spring Boot**: `4.0.3`（当前最新稳定版）
- **Spring AI**: `2.0.0-M2`（当前与 Spring Boot 4.x 兼容的最新版本，GA 正式版预计 2026 年 5 月底发布，届时建议升级）
- **JDK**: `21`（已启用虚拟线程）

需要注意的是，Spring AI 2.0 目前仍处于 Milestone 阶段（M2），还不是正式 GA 版本。这是因为 Spring Boot 4.x 刚发布不久，Spring AI 团队正在适配中。如果你需要使用完全稳定的组合，可以考虑退回到 **Spring Boot 3.5.x + Spring AI 1.0.x**。我已经在规则中标注了这一点，等 GA 版本发布后只需更新 BOM 版本号即可。

The user is asking two questions:

1. Is there any problem with setting all 4 rules to `alwaysApply: true`?
2. If keeping the original settings (not always apply), how do the non-always-apply rules work?

Let me explain how Cursor rules work with different settings. This is a knowledge question, so I'll answer directly.

In Cursor, rules have three modes:
- `alwaysApply: true` — Always included in every conversation context
- `alwaysApply: false` with `globs` — Automatically applied when files matching the glob pattern are open/being edited
- `alwaysApply: false` without `globs` — Manual, needs to be referenced by the user with @


The non-always-apply rules I created have `globs` set, so they'll automatically activate when you're working on matching files. This approach is better because it keeps your context window lean by only loading relevant rules, while still giving the AI focused guidance based on what you're actually editing. Setting everything to always apply would work, but it wastes context space on rules you might not need in every conversation.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

这是个很好的问题。两种方式都可以用，但有区别。让我解释一下。

## 全部设为 Always Apply 有没有问题？

**功能上没有问题**，完全可以这样做。但有一个代价：

- 每次你和 AI 对话时，**所有 4 个规则的内容都会被注入到上下文窗口中**，无论你当前在做什么
- 比如你只是在写 `README.md`，并没有编辑任何 Java 文件或 YAML 配置，Spring AI 的代码规范和配置规范也会占用上下文空间
- 对于目前只有 4 个规则来说影响很小，但随着项目规模增大、规则越来越多时，会浪费宝贵的上下文窗口

**结论：现阶段规则少，全部 Always Apply 完全没问题。**

---

## 我原来设置的方式是怎么工作的？

我设置了两种模式：

### 1. Always Apply（始终生效）
`spring-ai-project.mdc` — 每次对话都会自动加载，因为它是项目的总纲（技术栈、目录结构），无论你编辑什么文件都需要知道。

### 2. Globs 模式（按文件自动匹配）
其余 3 个规则设置了 `globs` 文件匹配模式：

| 规则 | globs | 何时自动生效 |
|------|-------|------------|
| `java-conventions.mdc` | `**/*.java` | 当你打开或编辑任何 `.java` 文件时 |
| `spring-ai-patterns.mdc` | `**/*.java` | 当你打开或编辑任何 `.java` 文件时 |
| `config-conventions.mdc` | `src/main/resources/**/*.{yml,yaml,properties,st}` | 当你打开或编辑配置文件时 |

**这些规则不需要你手动做任何事情。** 它们是全自动的 — Cursor 检测到你当前打开的文件匹配 `globs` 模式时，就会自动把对应规则注入到 AI 的上下文中。

举个例子：
- 你打开 `ChatService.java` 编辑 → Cursor 自动加载 `java-conventions.mdc` + `spring-ai-patterns.mdc`
- 你打开 `application.yml` 编辑 → Cursor 自动加载 `config-conventions.mdc`
- 你只是在聊天问问题，没打开文件 → 只有 `spring-ai-project.mdc` 生效

---

## 建议

如果你觉得规则数量不多、不想纠结这些细节，**全部设成 Always Apply 完全可以**，简单省心。

如果你想保持我原来的精细化设置（推荐），**不需要做任何额外操作**，Cursor 会根据你当前编辑的文件自动匹配加载，体验上是无感的。

## 提供的 API 接口

| 方法 | 路径 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| POST | `/api/chat` | 普通问答，阻塞式返回完整回答 |
| GET | `/api/chat/stream?message=你好` | 流式问答，SSE 逐 token 返回 |

## 启动前需要修改的内容

在 `src/main/resources/application.yml` 中修改以下两项：

```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    openai:
      api-key: sk-your-dashscope-api-key    # ← 替换为你的百炼 API Key
      chat:
        options:
          model: qwen-max                    # ← 替换为你要使用的模型名称
```

修改后执行 `mvn spring-boot:run` 即可启动项目，然后在浏览器中访问 `http://localhost:8080/api/chat/stream?message=你好` 即可体验流式问答。

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

好的，下面帮你详细分析。

---

## 先说结论：两个 starter 共存不会导致编码冲突

`spring-boot-starter-web` + `spring-boot-starter-webflux` 同时存在时，Spring Boot 检测到 Servlet 容器（Tomcat）会**自动选择 Servlet（MVC）模式**运行，WebFlux 只是作为一个库提供 `Flux`/`Mono` 等响应式类型。这不是乱码的根因。

---

## 真正的原因分析

乱码内容 `浣犲ソ锛佸緢楂樺叴瑙佸埌` 是典型的 **UTF-8 字节被当作 ISO-8859-1/Latin-1 解码** 的结果。问题出在 SSE 的响应链路中，`produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8"` 只解决了 HTTP 头的 Content-Type 声明，但实际还有几个环节可能出问题：

### 1. Tomcat 默认 Response 编码

Spring MVC 运行在 Tomcat 上，Tomcat 的 `HttpServletResponse` 默认编码可能不是 UTF-8。在 Spring Boot 4.x 中，虽然理论上已默认 UTF-8，但当 MVC 处理 `Flux<String>` 返回类型时，编码可能在序列化层被覆盖。

### 2. Spring MVC 处理 Flux 的内部机制

当 MVC 遇到 `Flux<String>` 返回值 + `text/event-stream` 时，会使用 `SseHttpMessageWriter` 或类似的处理器来写入响应。这个处理器在序列化 SSE `data:` 行时，可能用了平台默认编码而非 UTF-8。

### 3. `produces` 中 charset 可能没生效

在某些 Spring MVC 版本中，`produces` 里的 `charset` 部分会被忽略或被内部 `MediaType` 解析覆盖，特别是在 Servlet 模式下处理 reactive 返回类型这种"跨界"场景中。

---

## 解决方案建议

有几种方式可以尝试，按推荐优先级排列：

### 方案 A：去掉 `spring-boot-starter-web`，只用 `spring-boot-starter-webflux`

这是最干净的方案。让应用运行在纯 Netty + WebFlux 模式下，WebFlux 原生处理 `Flux<String>` + SSE，编码默认就是 UTF-8，不存在 Servlet 层的编码问题。

pom.xml 改动：去掉 `spring-boot-starter-web`，只保留 `spring-boot-starter-webflux`。代码无需任何修改，`@RestController`、`@GetMapping` 这些注解在 WebFlux 中同样生效。

### 方案 B：保留双 starter，强制全局 UTF-8 编码

在 `application.yml` 中增加：

```yaml
server:
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

`force-response: true` 会强制所有 Servlet 响应使用 UTF-8，不管 Content-Type 怎么协商。

### 方案 C：在 Controller 层手动设置 Response 编码

用 `HttpServletResponse` 显式设置编码：

```java
@GetMapping(value = "/stream", produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8")
public Flux<String> streamChat(@RequestParam String message, HttpServletResponse response) {
    response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8");
    return chatService.streamChat(message);
}
```

---

## 推荐

**方案 A** 最彻底 — 你这个项目目前没有用到任何 Servlet 专属功能（如 Filter、HttpSession 等），纯 WebFlux 模式完全够用，而且 SSE 流式输出本身就是 WebFlux 的强项。

如果以后需要加传统 MVC 功能，再换回双 starter + 方案 B 即可。

需要我帮你改的话，切换到 Agent 模式即可。

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about the `server.servlet.encoding` configuration properties in Spring Boot - specifically `charset`, `force`, and `force-response`. Let me explain what each one does.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

这三个配置属于 Spring Boot 的 **Servlet 字符编码过滤器**（`CharacterEncodingFilter`）的控制项，用来解决 HTTP 请求和响应的编码问题。

---

## 逐个解释

### `charset: UTF-8`

指定字符编码为 UTF-8。这个值会被设置到 `CharacterEncodingFilter` 上，作为 Servlet 容器处理请求/响应时的默认编码。

- 影响 `request.getCharacterEncoding()`
- 影响 `response.getCharacterEncoding()`

### `force: true`

**强制覆盖请求（Request）的编码**。

- 默认是 `true`
- 当设为 `true` 时，即使客户端在请求头 `Content-Type` 中指定了其他编码（比如 `Content-Type: application/json; charset=GBK`），Spring 也会**无视它**，强制用 `charset` 指定的 UTF-8 来解析请求体
- 设为 `false` 时，只有在客户端没有指定编码的情况下才使用 UTF-8 作为默认值

### `force-response: true`

**强制覆盖响应（Response）的编码**。这是解决你乱码问题的关键。

- 默认是 `false`
- 当设为 `true` 时，不管框架内部或其他组件把 `response.setCharacterEncoding()` 设成了什么值，最终都会被**强制改回 UTF-8**
- 设为 `false` 时，只在响应没有显式设置编码的情况下才用 UTF-8

---

## 工作原理

这三个配置实际上控制的是 Spring Boot 自动注册的 `CharacterEncodingFilter`，它是 Servlet Filter 链中最早执行的过滤器之一：

```
客户端请求 → CharacterEncodingFilter → Controller → 响应输出
                  ↓
          force=true → 强制 request 编码为 UTF-8
          force-response=true → 强制 response 编码为 UTF-8
```

---

## 和你的乱码问题的关系

你的乱码场景是这样的：

1. `ChatService` 从阿里百炼拿到的数据是 **UTF-8 编码的中文**
2. 数据通过 `Flux<String>` 传到 SSE 响应
3. Tomcat 在写入 HTTP 响应体时，用了**非 UTF-8 编码**（可能是 ISO-8859-1）去序列化字符串
4. 浏览器收到的字节已经"损坏"了，所以显示乱码

加上 `force-response: true` 后，`CharacterEncodingFilter` 会在响应写出之前把编码锁定为 UTF-8，从而避免 Tomcat 内部用错误编码。

---

## 一句话总结

| 配置 | 作用对象 | 默认值 | 一句话 |
|------|---------|--------|--------|
| `charset` | 请求+响应 | UTF-8 | 指定用什么编码 |
| `force` | 请求 | true | 无视客户端声明的编码，强制用 charset |
| `force-response` | 响应 | **false** | 无视框架内部设置的编码，强制用 charset |

你的问题本质就是 `force-response` 默认为 `false`，导致响应编码没有被强制锁定为 UTF-8。

我当前处于 Ask 模式，无法直接编辑文件。但修复方式很简单：

## 原因

Spring Boot 4.0 把 `server.servlet.encoding.*` 这组配置**迁移到了** `spring.servlet.encoding.*` 下，旧路径已被标记为 Deprecated。

## 修复方式

把第 1-7 行从：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

改为：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080

spring:
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

注意 `encoding` 那三行要移到已有的 `spring:` 节点下面，不要重复写两个 `spring:`。合并后的完整文件应该是：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080

spring:
  application:
    name: my-ai-cursor

  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true

  threads:
    virtual:
      enabled: true

  ai:
    openai:
      base-url: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode
      api-key: sk-537fe04bb20444758dd760331a8e599b
      chat:
        options:
          model: qwen3.5-plus
          temperature: 0.7
          max-tokens: 2048
```

这是 Spring Boot 4.0 的迁移变更之一 — 把原来挂在 `server` 下的 Servlet 编码配置归到了 `spring` 命名空间下，使配置层级更统一。功能完全一样，只是路径变了。

[MariaDB ベクトルストア :: Spring AI リファレンス - ドキュメント](https://spring.pleiades.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs/mariadb.html)\n\n# Spring AI MariaDB VectorStore Support in 2.0.0-M2\n\nSpring AI version 2.0.0-M2 includes support for MariaDB as a VectorStore.[1]\n\n## Key Features\n\n**MariaDB Vector Requirements:** MariaDB 11.7+ is required, as MariaDB Vector enables storing and searching machine learning-generated embeddings with efficient vector similarity search using vector indexes. It supports both cosine similarity and Euclidean distance metrics.[2]\n\n**Spring Boot 4.x Compatibility:** Spring AI 2.x.x (including 2.0.0-M2) requires Spring Boot 4.x.[1]\n\n## Setup\n\nTo enable MariaDB VectorStore, add the following dependency:[2]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;xml\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-starter-vector-store-mariadb\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAdditionally, you need a configured &#96;EmbeddingModel&#96; bean, such as the OpenAI EmbeddingModel starter.[2]\n\n## Important Configuration Changes\n\n**Schema Initialization:** Unlike earlier Spring AI versions where schema initialization happened by default, version 2.0.0-M2 requires opt-in by setting &#96;initialize-schema=true&#96; in your configuration or constructor.[2]\n\n## Basic Usage\n\nOnce configured, you can auto-wire the &#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96; to add documents and perform similarity searches:[2]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;java\n@Autowired VectorStore vectorStore;\nvectorStore.add(documents);\nList\u003cDocument\u003e results = vectorStore.similaritySearch(SearchRequest.builder().query(\"Spring\").topK(5).build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",

```json
{
  "markdown": "MariaDB Vector Store :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# MariaDB Vector Store\n\nThis section walks you through setting up&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96; to store document embeddings and perform similarity searches.\n\n[MariaDB Vector](https://mariadb.org/projects/mariadb-vector/) is part of MariaDB 11.7 and enables storing and searching over machine learning-generated embeddings. It provides efficient vector similarity search capabilities using vector indexes, supporting both cosine similarity and Euclidean distance metrics.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nA running MariaDB (11.7+) instance. The following options are available:\n\n[Docker](https://hub.docker.com/_/mariadb) image\n\n[MariaDB Server](https://mariadb.org/download/)\n\n[MariaDB SkySQL](https://mariadb.com/products/skysql/)\n\nIf required, an API key for the [EmbeddingModel](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings.html#available-implementations) to generate the embeddings stored by the&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96;.\n\n## Auto-Configuration\n\nThere has been a significant change in the Spring AI auto-configuration, starter modules' artifact names. Please refer to the [upgrade notes](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/upgrade-notes.html) for more information.\n\nSpring AI provides Spring Boot auto-configuration for the MariaDB Vector Store. To enable it, add the following dependency to your project’s Maven&#96;pom.xml&#96; file:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-starter-vector-store-mariadb\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nor to your Gradle&#96;build.gradle&#96; build file.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n    implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-starter-vector-store-mariadb'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Refer to the [Dependency Management](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html#dependency-management) section to add the Spring AI BOM to your build file. |\n| --- |\n\nThe vector store implementation can initialize the required schema for you, but you must opt-in by specifying the&#96;initializeSchema&#96; boolean in the appropriate constructor or by setting&#96;…​initialize-schema=true&#96; in the&#96;application.properties&#96; file.\n\n| This is a breaking change! In earlier versions of Spring AI, this schema initialization happened by default. |\n| --- |\n\nAdditionally, you will need a configured&#96;EmbeddingModel&#96; bean. Refer to the [EmbeddingModel](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings.html#available-implementations) section for more information.\n\nFor example, to use the [OpenAI EmbeddingModel](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings/openai-embeddings.html), add the following dependency:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-starter-model-openai\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Refer to the [Artifact Repositories](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html#artifact-repositories) section to add Maven Central and/or Snapshot Repositories to your build file. |\n| --- |\n\nNow you can auto-wire the&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96; in your application:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Autowired VectorStore vectorStore;\n\n// ...\n\nList\u003cDocument\u003e documents = List.of(\n    new Document(\"Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!!\", Map.of(\"meta1\", \"meta1\")),\n    new Document(\"The World is Big and Salvation Lurks Around the Corner\"),\n    new Document(\"You walk forward facing the past and you turn back toward the future.\", Map.of(\"meta2\", \"meta2\")));\n\n// Add the documents to MariaDB\nvectorStore.add(documents);\n\n// Retrieve documents similar to a query\nList\u003cDocument\u003e results = vectorStore.similaritySearch(SearchRequest.builder().query(\"Spring\").topK(5).build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Configuration Properties\n\nTo connect to MariaDB and use the&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96;, you need to provide access details for your instance. A simple configuration can be provided via Spring Boot’s&#96;application.yml&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nspring:\n  datasource:\n    url: jdbc:mariadb://localhost/db\n    username: myUser\n    password: myPassword\n  ai:\n    vectorstore:\n      mariadb:\n        initialize-schema: true\n        distance-type: COSINE\n        dimensions: 1536\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| If you run MariaDB Vector as a Spring Boot dev service via [Docker Compose](https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/reference/features/dev-services.html#features.dev-services.docker-compose) or [Testcontainers](https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/reference/features/dev-services.html#features.dev-services.testcontainers), you don’t need to configure URL, username and password since they are autoconfigured by Spring Boot. |\n| --- |\n\nProperties starting with&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.*&#96; are used to configure the&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96;:\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.initialize-schema&#96;\n\nWhether to initialize the required schema\n\n&#96;false&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.distance-type&#96;\n\nSearch distance type. Use&#96;COSINE&#96;(default) or&#96;EUCLIDEAN&#96;. If vectors are normalized to length 1, you can use&#96;EUCLIDEAN&#96; for best performance.\n\n&#96;COSINE&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.dimensions&#96;\n\nEmbeddings dimension. If not specified explicitly, will retrieve dimensions from the provided&#96;EmbeddingModel&#96;.\n\n&#96;1536&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.remove-existing-vector-store-table&#96;\n\nDeletes the existing vector store table on startup.\n\n&#96;false&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.schema-name&#96;\n\nVector store schema name\n\n&#96;null&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.table-name&#96;\n\nVector store table name\n\n&#96;vector_store&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.schema-validation&#96;\n\nEnables schema and table name validation to ensure they are valid and existing objects.\n\n&#96;false&#96;\n\n| Property | Description | Default Value |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n\n| If you configure a custom schema and/or table name, consider enabling schema validation by setting&#96;spring.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.schema-validation=true&#96;. This ensures the correctness of the names and reduces the risk of SQL injection attacks. |\n| --- |\n\n## Manual Configuration\n\nInstead of using the Spring Boot auto-configuration, you can manually configure the MariaDB vector store. For this you need to add the following dependencies to your project:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.boot\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-boot-starter-jdbc\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.mariadb.jdbc\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003emariadb-java-client\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n    \u003cscope\u003eruntime\u003c/scope\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-mariadb-store\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Refer to the [Dependency Management](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html#dependency-management) section to add the Spring AI BOM to your build file. |\n| --- |\n\nThen create the&#96;MariaDBVectorStore&#96; bean using the builder pattern:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@Bean\npublic VectorStore vectorStore(JdbcTemplate jdbcTemplate, EmbeddingModel embeddingModel) {\n    return MariaDBVectorStore.builder(jdbcTemplate, embeddingModel)\n        .dimensions(1536)                      // Optional: defaults to 1536\n        .distanceType(MariaDBDistanceType.COSINE) // Optional: defaults to COSINE\n        .schemaName(\"mydb\")                    // Optional: defaults to null\n        .vectorTableName(\"custom_vectors\")     // Optional: defaults to \"vector_store\"\n        .contentFieldName(\"text\")             // Optional: defaults to \"content\"\n        .embeddingFieldName(\"embedding\")      // Optional: defaults to \"embedding\"\n        .idFieldName(\"doc_id\")                // Optional: defaults to \"id\"\n        .metadataFieldName(\"meta\")           // Optional: defaults to \"metadata\"\n        .initializeSchema(true)               // Optional: defaults to false\n        .schemaValidation(true)              // Optional: defaults to false\n        .removeExistingVectorStoreTable(false) // Optional: defaults to false\n        .maxDocumentBatchSize(10000)         // Optional: defaults to 10000\n        .build();\n}\n\n// This can be any EmbeddingModel implementation\n@Bean\npublic EmbeddingModel embeddingModel() {\n    return new OpenAiEmbeddingModel(new OpenAiApi(System.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")));\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Metadata Filtering\n\nYou can leverage the generic, portable [metadata filters](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs.html#metadata-filters) with MariaDB Vector store.\n\nFor example, you can use either the text expression language:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nvectorStore.similaritySearch(\n    SearchRequest.builder()\n        .query(\"The World\")\n        .topK(TOP_K)\n        .similarityThreshold(SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD)\n        .filterExpression(\"author in ['john', 'jill'] \u0026\u0026 article_type == 'blog'\").build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nor programmatically using the&#96;Filter.Expression&#96; DSL:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nFilterExpressionBuilder b = new FilterExpressionBuilder();\n\nvectorStore.similaritySearch(SearchRequest.builder()\n    .query(\"The World\")\n    .topK(TOP_K)\n    .similarityThreshold(SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD)\n    .filterExpression(b.and(\n        b.in(\"author\", \"john\", \"jill\"),\n        b.eq(\"article_type\", \"blog\")).build()).build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| These filter expressions are automatically converted into the equivalent MariaDB JSON path expressions. |\n| --- |\n\n## Similarity Scores\n\nThe MariaDB Vector Store automatically calculates similarity scores for documents returned from similarity searches. These scores provide a normalized measure of how closely each document matches your search query.\n\n### Score Calculation\n\nSimilarity scores are calculated using the formula&#96;score = 1.0 - distance&#96;, where:\n\nScore: A value between&#96;0.0&#96; and&#96;1.0&#96;, where&#96;1.0&#96; indicates perfect similarity and&#96;0.0&#96; indicates no similarity\n\nDistance: The raw distance value calculated using the configured distance type (&#96;COSINE&#96; or&#96;EUCLIDEAN&#96;)\n\nThis means that documents with smaller distances (more similar) will have higher scores, making the results more intuitive to interpret.\n\n### Accessing Scores\n\nYou can access the similarity score for each document through the&#96;getScore()&#96; method:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nList\u003cDocument\u003e results = vectorStore.similaritySearch(\n    SearchRequest.builder()\n        .query(\"Spring AI\")\n        .topK(5)\n        .build());\n\nfor (Document doc : results) {\n    double score = doc.getScore();  // Value between 0.0 and 1.0\n    System.out.println(\"Document: \" + doc.getText());\n    System.out.println(\"Similarity Score: \" + score);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Search Results Ordering\n\nSearch results are automatically ordered by similarity score in descending order (highest score first). This ensures that the most relevant documents appear at the top of your results.\n\n### Distance Metadata\n\nIn addition to the similarity score, the raw distance value is still available in the document metadata:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfor (Document doc : results) {\n    double score = doc.getScore();\n    float distance = (Float) doc.getMetadata().get(\"distance\");\n\n    System.out.println(\"Score: \" + score + \", Distance: \" + distance);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Similarity Threshold\n\nWhen using similarity thresholds in your search requests, specify the threshold as a score value (&#96;0.0&#96; to&#96;1.0&#96;) rather than a distance:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nList\u003cDocument\u003e results = vectorStore.similaritySearch(\n    SearchRequest.builder()\n        .query(\"Spring AI\")\n        .topK(10)\n        .similarityThreshold(0.8)  // Only return documents with score \u003e= 0.8\n        .build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis makes threshold values consistent and intuitive - higher values mean more restrictive searches that only return highly similar documents.\n\n## Accessing the Native Client\n\nThe MariaDB Vector Store implementation provides access to the underlying native JDBC client (&#96;JdbcTemplate&#96;) through the&#96;getNativeClient()&#96; method:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMariaDBVectorStore vectorStore = context.getBean(MariaDBVectorStore.class);\nOptional\u003cJdbcTemplate\u003e nativeClient = vectorStore.getNativeClient();\n\nif (nativeClient.isPresent()) {\n    JdbcTemplate jdbc = nativeClient.get();\n    // Use the native client for MariaDB-specific operations\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe native client gives you access to MariaDB-specific features and operations that might not be exposed through the&#96;VectorStore&#96; interface.",
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs/mariadb.html"
}
```

```json
{
  "markdown": "Ollama Embeddings :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Ollama Embeddings\n\nWith [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) you can run various [AI Models](https://ollama.com/search?c=embedding) locally and generate embeddings from them. An embedding is a vector (list) of floating point numbers. The distance between two vectors measures their relatedness. Small distances suggest high relatedness and large distances suggest low relatedness.\n\nThe&#96;OllamaEmbeddingModel&#96; implementation leverages the Ollama [Embeddings API](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-embeddings) endpoint.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nYou first need access to an Ollama instance. There are a few options, including the following:\n\n[Download and install Ollama](https://ollama.com/download) on your local machine.\n\nConfigure and [run Ollama via Testcontainers](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/testcontainers.html).\n\nBind to an Ollama instance via [Kubernetes Service Bindings](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/cloud-bindings.html).\n\nYou can pull the models you want to use in your application from the [Ollama model library](https://ollama.com/search?c=embedding):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nollama pull \u003cmodel-name\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also pull any of the thousands, free, [GGUF Hugging Face Models](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf\u0026amp;sort=trending):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nollama pull hf.co/\u003cusername\u003e/\u003cmodel-repository\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAlternatively, you can enable the option to download automatically any needed model: Auto-pulling Models.\n\n## Auto-configuration\n\nThere has been a significant change in the Spring AI auto-configuration, starter modules' artifact names. Please refer to the [upgrade notes](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/upgrade-notes.html) for more information.\n\nSpring AI provides Spring Boot auto-configuration for the Azure Ollama Embedding Model. To enable it add the following dependency to your Maven&#96;pom.xml&#96; or Gradle&#96;build.gradle&#96; build files:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n   \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n   \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-starter-model-ollama\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n    implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-starter-model-ollama'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Refer to the [Dependency Management](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html#dependency-management) section to add the Spring AI BOM to your build file. Spring AI artifacts are published in Maven Central and Spring Snapshot repositories. Refer to the Repositories section to add these repositories to your build system. |\n| --- |\n\n### Base Properties\n\nThe prefix&#96;spring.ai.ollama&#96; is the property prefix to configure the connection to Ollama\n\nProperty\n\nDescription\n\nDefault\n\nspring.ai.ollama.base-url\n\nBase URL where Ollama API server is running.\n\n&#96;http://localhost:11434&#96;\n\nHere are the properties for initializing the Ollama integration and auto-pulling models.\n\nProperty\n\nDescription\n\nDefault\n\nspring.ai.ollama.init.pull-model-strategy\n\nWhether to pull models at startup-time and how.\n\n&#96;never&#96;\n\nspring.ai.ollama.init.timeout\n\nHow long to wait for a model to be pulled.\n\n&#96;5m&#96;\n\nspring.ai.ollama.init.max-retries\n\nMaximum number of retries for the model pull operation.\n\n&#96;0&#96;\n\nspring.ai.ollama.init.embedding.include\n\nInclude this type of models in the initialization task.\n\n&#96;true&#96;\n\nspring.ai.ollama.init.embedding.additional-models\n\nAdditional models to initialize besides the ones configured via default properties.\n\n&#96;[]&#96;\n\n### Embedding Properties\n\nEnabling and disabling of the embedding auto-configurations are now configured via top level properties with the prefix&#96;spring.ai.model.embedding&#96;.\n\nTo enable, spring.ai.model.embedding=ollama (It is enabled by default)\n\nTo disable, spring.ai.model.embedding=none (or any value which doesn’t match ollama)\n\nThis change is done to allow configuration of multiple models.\n\nThe prefix&#96;spring.ai.ollama.embedding.options&#96; is the property prefix that configures the Ollama embedding model. It includes the Ollama request (advanced) parameters such as the&#96;model&#96;,&#96;keep-alive&#96;, and&#96;truncate&#96; as well as the Ollama model&#96;options&#96; properties.\n\nHere are the advanced request parameter for the Ollama embedding model:\n\nProperty\n\nDescription\n\nDefault\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.enabled (Removed and no longer valid)\n\nEnables the Ollama embedding model auto-configuration.\n\ntrue\n\nspring.ai.model.embedding\n\nEnables the Ollama embedding model auto-configuration.\n\nollama\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.model\n\nThe name of the [supported model](https://github.com/ollama/ollama?tab=readme-ov-file#model-library) to use. You can use dedicated [Embedding Model](https://ollama.com/search?c=embedding) types\n\nmxbai-embed-large\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.keep_alive\n\nControls how long the model will stay loaded into memory following the request\n\n5m\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.truncate\n\nTruncates the end of each input to fit within context length. Returns error if false and context length is exceeded.\n\ntrue\n\nThe remaining&#96;options&#96; properties are based on the [Ollama Valid Parameters and Values](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/modelfile.md#valid-parameters-and-values) and [Ollama Types](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/api/types.go). The default values are based on: [Ollama type defaults](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/b538dc3858014f94b099730a592751a5454cab0a/api/types.go#L364).\n\nProperty\n\nDescription\n\nDefault\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.numa\n\nWhether to use NUMA.\n\nfalse\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-ctx\n\nSets the size of the context window used to generate the next token.\n\n2048\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-batch\n\nPrompt processing maximum batch size.\n\n512\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-gpu\n\nThe number of layers to send to the GPU(s). On macOS it defaults to 1 to enable metal support, 0 to disable. 1 here indicates that NumGPU should be set dynamically\n\n-1\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.main-gpu\n\nWhen using multiple GPUs this option controls which GPU is used for small tensors for which the overhead of splitting the computation across all GPUs is not worthwhile. The GPU in question will use slightly more VRAM to store a scratch buffer for temporary results.\n\n0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.low-vram\n\n-\n\nfalse\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.f16-kv\n\n-\n\ntrue\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.logits-all\n\nReturn logits for all the tokens, not just the last one. To enable completions to return logprobs, this must be true.\n\n-\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.vocab-only\n\nLoad only the vocabulary, not the weights.\n\n-\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.use-mmap\n\nBy default, models are mapped into memory, which allows the system to load only the necessary parts of the model as needed. However, if the model is larger than your total amount of RAM or if your system is low on available memory, using mmap might increase the risk of pageouts, negatively impacting performance. Disabling mmap results in slower load times but may reduce pageouts if you’re not using mlock. Note that if the model is larger than the total amount of RAM, turning off mmap would prevent the model from loading at all.\n\nnull\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.use-mlock\n\nLock the model in memory, preventing it from being swapped out when memory-mapped. This can improve performance but trades away some of the advantages of memory-mapping by requiring more RAM to run and potentially slowing down load times as the model loads into RAM.\n\nfalse\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-thread\n\nSets the number of threads to use during computation. By default, Ollama will detect this for optimal performance. It is recommended to set this value to the number of physical CPU cores your system has (as opposed to the logical number of cores). 0 = let the runtime decide\n\n0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-keep\n\n-\n\n4\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.seed\n\nSets the random number seed to use for generation. Setting this to a specific number will make the model generate the same text for the same prompt.\n\n-1\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.num-predict\n\nMaximum number of tokens to predict when generating text. (-1 = infinite generation, -2 = fill context)\n\n-1\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.top-k\n\nReduces the probability of generating nonsense. A higher value (e.g., 100) will give more diverse answers, while a lower value (e.g., 10) will be more conservative.\n\n40\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.top-p\n\nWorks together with top-k. A higher value (e.g., 0.95) will lead to more diverse text, while a lower value (e.g., 0.5) will generate more focused and conservative text.\n\n0.9\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.min-p\n\nAlternative to the top_p, and aims to ensure a balance of quality and variety. The parameter p represents the minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. For example, with p=0.05 and the most likely token having a probability of 0.9, logits with a value less than 0.045 are filtered out.\n\n0.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.tfs-z\n\nTail-free sampling is used to reduce the impact of less probable tokens from the output. A higher value (e.g., 2.0) will reduce the impact more, while a value of 1.0 disables this setting.\n\n1.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.typical-p\n\n-\n\n1.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.repeat-last-n\n\nSets how far back for the model to look back to prevent repetition. (Default: 64, 0 = disabled, -1 = num_ctx)\n\n64\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.temperature\n\nThe temperature of the model. Increasing the temperature will make the model answer more creatively.\n\n0.8\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.repeat-penalty\n\nSets how strongly to penalize repetitions. A higher value (e.g., 1.5) will penalize repetitions more strongly, while a lower value (e.g., 0.9) will be more lenient.\n\n1.1\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.presence-penalty\n\n-\n\n0.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.frequency-penalty\n\n-\n\n0.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.mirostat\n\nEnable Mirostat sampling for controlling perplexity. (default: 0, 0 = disabled, 1 = Mirostat, 2 = Mirostat 2.0)\n\n0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.mirostat-tau\n\nControls the balance between coherence and diversity of the output. A lower value will result in more focused and coherent text.\n\n5.0\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.mirostat-eta\n\nInfluences how quickly the algorithm responds to feedback from the generated text. A lower learning rate will result in slower adjustments, while a higher learning rate will make the algorithm more responsive.\n\n0.1\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.penalize-newline\n\n-\n\ntrue\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.stop\n\nSets the stop sequences to use. When this pattern is encountered the LLM will stop generating text and return. Multiple stop patterns may be set by specifying multiple separate stop parameters in a modelfile.\n\n-\n\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.functions\n\nList of functions, identified by their names, to enable for function calling in a single prompt requests. Functions with those names must exist in the functionCallbacks registry.\n\n-\n\n| All properties prefixed with&#96;spring.ai.ollama.embedding.options&#96; can be overridden at runtime by adding a request specific Runtime Options to the&#96;EmbeddingRequest&#96; call. |\n| --- |\n\n## Runtime Options\n\nThe [OllamaEmbeddingOptions.java](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/models/spring-ai-ollama/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/ollama/api/OllamaEmbeddingOptions.java) provides the Ollama configurations, such as the model to use, the low level GPU and CPU tuning, etc.\n\n| The&#96;OllamaOptions&#96; class has been deprecated. Use&#96;OllamaChatOptions&#96; for chat models and&#96;OllamaEmbeddingOptions&#96; for embedding models instead. The new classes provide type-safe, model-specific configuration options. |\n| --- |\n\nThe default options can be configured using the&#96;spring.ai.ollama.embedding.options&#96; properties as well.\n\nAt start-time use the&#96;OllamaEmbeddingModel(OllamaApi ollamaApi, OllamaEmbeddingOptions defaultOptions)&#96; to configure the default options used for all embedding requests. At run-time you can override the default options, using a&#96;OllamaEmbeddingOptions&#96; instance as part of your&#96;EmbeddingRequest&#96;.\n\nFor example to override the default model name for a specific request:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nEmbeddingResponse embeddingResponse = embeddingModel.call(\n    new EmbeddingRequest(List.of(\"Hello World\", \"World is big and salvation is near\"),\n        OllamaEmbeddingOptions.builder()\n            .model(\"Different-Embedding-Model-Deployment-Name\"))\n            .truncates(false)\n            .build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Auto-pulling Models\n\nSpring AI Ollama can automatically pull models when they are not available in your Ollama instance. This feature is particularly useful for development and testing as well as for deploying your applications to new environments.\n\n| You can also pull, by name, any of the thousands, free, [GGUF Hugging Face Models](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf\u0026amp;sort=trending). |\n| --- |\n\nThere are three strategies for pulling models:\n\n&#96;always&#96;(defined in&#96;PullModelStrategy.ALWAYS&#96;): Always pull the model, even if it’s already available. Useful to ensure you’re using the latest version of the model.\n\n&#96;when_missing&#96;(defined in&#96;PullModelStrategy.WHEN_MISSING&#96;): Only pull the model if it’s not already available. This may result in using an older version of the model.\n\n&#96;never&#96;(defined in&#96;PullModelStrategy.NEVER&#96;): Never pull the model automatically.\n\n| Due to potential delays while downloading models, automatic pulling is not recommended for production environments. Instead, consider assessing and pre-downloading the necessary models in advance. |\n| --- |\n\nAll models defined via configuration properties and default options can be automatically pulled at startup time. You can configure the pull strategy, timeout, and maximum number of retries using configuration properties:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nspring:\n  ai:\n    ollama:\n      init:\n        pull-model-strategy: always\n        timeout: 60s\n        max-retries: 1\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| The application will not complete its initialization until all specified models are available in Ollama. Depending on the model size and internet connection speed, this may significantly slow down your application’s startup time. |\n| --- |\n\nYou can initialize additional models at startup, which is useful for models used dynamically at runtime:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nspring:\n  ai:\n    ollama:\n      init:\n        pull-model-strategy: always\n        embedding:\n          additional-models:\n            - mxbai-embed-large\n            - nomic-embed-text\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you want to apply the pulling strategy only to specific types of models, you can exclude embedding models from the initialization task:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nspring:\n  ai:\n    ollama:\n      init:\n        pull-model-strategy: always\n        embedding:\n          include: false\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis configuration will apply the pulling strategy to all models except embedding models.\n\n## HuggingFace Models\n\nOllama can access, out of the box, all [GGUF Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf\u0026amp;sort=trending) Embedding models. You can pull any of these models by name:&#96;ollama pull hf.co/ / &#96; or configure the auto-pulling strategy: Auto-pulling Models:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nspring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.model=hf.co/mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1\nspring.ai.ollama.init.pull-model-strategy=always\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;spring.ai.ollama.embedding.options.model&#96;: Specifies the [Hugging Face GGUF model](https://huggingface.co/models?library=gguf\u0026amp;sort=trending) to use.\n\n&#96;spring.ai.ollama.init.pull-model-strategy=always&#96;: (optional) Enables automatic model pulling at startup time. For production, you should pre-download the models to avoid delays:&#96;ollama pull hf.co/mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1&#96;.\n\n## Sample Controller\n\nThis will create a&#96;EmbeddingModel&#96; implementation that you can inject into your class. Here is an example of a simple&#96;@Controller&#96; class that uses the&#96;EmbeddingModel&#96; implementation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@RestController\npublic class EmbeddingController {\n\n    private final EmbeddingModel embeddingModel;\n\n    @Autowired\n    public EmbeddingController(EmbeddingModel embeddingModel) {\n        this.embeddingModel = embeddingModel;\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/ai/embedding\")\n    public Map embed(@RequestParam(value = \"message\", defaultValue = \"Tell me a joke\") String message) {\n        EmbeddingResponse embeddingResponse = this.embeddingModel.embedForResponse(List.of(message));\n        return Map.of(\"embedding\", embeddingResponse);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Manual Configuration\n\nIf you are not using Spring Boot, you can manually configure the&#96;OllamaEmbeddingModel&#96;. For this add the spring-ai-ollama dependency to your project’s Maven pom.xml or Gradle&#96;build.gradle&#96; build files:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003cdependency\u003e\n    \u003cgroupId\u003eorg.springframework.ai\u003c/groupId\u003e\n    \u003cartifactId\u003espring-ai-ollama\u003c/artifactId\u003e\n\u003c/dependency\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n    implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-ollama'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Refer to the [Dependency Management](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html#dependency-management) section to add the Spring AI BOM to your build file. |\n| --- |\n\n| The&#96;spring-ai-ollama&#96; dependency provides access also to the&#96;OllamaChatModel&#96;. For more information about the&#96;OllamaChatModel&#96; refer to the [Ollama Chat Client](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/ollama-chat.html) section. |\n| --- |\n\nNext, create an&#96;OllamaEmbeddingModel&#96; instance and use it to compute the embeddings for two input texts using a dedicated&#96;chroma/all-minilm-l6-v2-f32&#96; embedding models:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nvar ollamaApi = OllamaApi.builder().build();\n\nvar embeddingModel = new OllamaEmbeddingModel(this.ollamaApi,\n        OllamaEmbeddingOptions.builder()\n\t\t\t.model(OllamaModel.MISTRAL.id())\n            .build());\n\nEmbeddingResponse embeddingResponse = this.embeddingModel.call(\n    new EmbeddingRequest(List.of(\"Hello World\", \"World is big and salvation is near\"),\n        OllamaEmbeddingOptions.builder()\n            .model(\"chroma/all-minilm-l6-v2-f32\"))\n            .truncate(false)\n            .build());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;OllamaEmbeddingOptions&#96; provides the configuration information for all embedding requests.",
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings/ollama-embeddings.html"
}
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

你贴的报错就是 **ChatModel 冲突**，不是代码逻辑问题：

- `spring-ai-starter-model-openai` 会注册 `openAiChatModel`
- `spring-ai-starter-model-ollama` 会注册 `ollamaChatModel`
- `ChatClientAutoConfiguration` 只接受一个默认 `ChatModel`，结果发现两个就启动失败

我已经按你这个方案（聊天走百炼、向量走 Ollama）做了配置修正，在 `application.yml` 里加了：

```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    model:
      chat: openai
      embedding: ollama
```

这样就明确指定：
- Chat -> `openAiChatModel`
- Embedding -> `ollama`（`bge-m3`）

你现在重启应用即可，这个 `2 beans found` 的报错会消失。  
另外那个 `spring.flyway` 的 IDE 提示只是配置元数据告警，不影响运行。

---

_**User**_

@c:\Users\刘强\.cursor\projects\e-studyProject-my-ai-cursor\terminals\2.txt:353-381 

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

这个报错是启动期依赖注入问题：`IngestionService` 构造器要求注入 `ObjectMapper`，但当前上下文没有该 Bean。  
我会把它改成服务内自建 `ObjectMapper`，避免依赖容器注入。

你这个接口名写错了，当前项目里是 **`/api/kb/ingest`**，不是 `/api/kb/ingestd`。  
`KnowledgeController` 里定义的是：

- `POST /api/kb/ingest`
- 入参字段来自 `IngestRequest`：`title`、`sourceUrl`、`sourceOrg`、`docType`、`content`

你这个场景（只处理 URL）可以这样调用：

## cURL 示例

```bash
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8887/api/kb/ingest" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "成人高血压食养指南（2023年版）",
    "sourceUrl": "https://www.nhc.gov.cn/sps/c100088/202301/f01895a06c5349ef999f25da833c166d/files/1732844468193_68545.pdf",
    "sourceOrg": "国家卫生健康委员会",
    "docType": "guideline"
  }'
```

## PowerShell 示例（你是 Windows）

```powershell
$body = @{
  title     = "成人高血压食养指南（2023年版）"
  sourceUrl = "https://www.nhc.gov.cn/sps/c100088/202301/f01895a06c5349ef999f25da833c166d/files/1732844468193_68545.pdf"
  sourceOrg = "国家卫生健康委员会"
  docType   = "guideline"
} | ConvertTo-Json

Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post `
  -Uri "http://localhost:8887/api/kb/ingest" `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body $body
```

## 说明

- `content` 可不传；服务会自动从 `sourceUrl` 抓取内容（PDF 会走 PDF 解析）。
- 返回体是 `IngestResponse`，大致类似：
  - `documentId`
  - `chunkCount`
  - `checksum`
  - `duplicated`
  - `message`

这个问题能修，根因在你现在的 PDF 下载方式：

```116:139:src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/service/IngestionService.java
private String fetchContentFromUrl(String sourceUrl) {
    try {
        if (sourceUrl.endsWith(".pdf")) {
            return fetchPdfText(sourceUrl);
        }
        return Jsoup.connect(sourceUrl)
                .timeout(60_000)
                .get()
                .text();
    } catch (IOException e) {
        throw new IllegalStateException("Failed to fetch content from url: " + sourceUrl, e);
    }
}

private String fetchPdfText(String sourceUrl) throws IOException {
    URI uri = URI.create(sourceUrl);
    try (InputStream inputStream = uri.toURL().openStream();
         PDDocument pdDocument = PDDocument.load(inputStream)) {
        return new PDFTextStripper().getText(pdDocument);
    }catch (Exception e) {
        e.printStackTrace();
    }
    return null;
}
```

`uri.toURL().openStream()` 在这类政府站点上经常触发 TLS 握手失败（你日志里的 `SSLHandshakeException`）。  
而且这里还把异常吞掉并返回 `null`，会让问题更隐蔽。

---

## 建议修复（推荐）

我现在是 Ask 模式不能直接改代码，你可以按下面改：

1. **用 `java.net.http.HttpClient` 下载 PDF**（替代 `openStream`）
2. 设置请求头：`User-Agent`、`Accept: application/pdf`
3. 启用重定向：`HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL`
4. 失败时直接抛异常，不要 `printStackTrace + return null`

示例实现（可直接替换 `fetchPdfText`）：

```java
private String fetchPdfText(String sourceUrl) {
    try {
        HttpClient client = HttpClient.newBuilder()
                .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL)
                .connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(30))
                .build();

        HttpRequest request = HttpRequest.newBuilder(URI.create(sourceUrl))
                .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(60))
                .header("User-Agent", "Mozilla/5.0")
                .header("Accept", "application/pdf,*/*")
                .GET()
                .build();

        HttpResponse<byte[]> response = client.send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofByteArray());
        if (response.statusCode() < 200 || response.statusCode() >= 300) {
            throw new IllegalStateException("Download pdf failed, status=" + response.statusCode());
        }

        try (PDDocument pdDocument = PDDocument.load(response.body())) {
            return new PDFTextStripper().getText(pdDocument);
        }
    } catch (Exception e) {
        throw new IllegalStateException("Failed to fetch PDF from url: " + sourceUrl, e);
    }
}
```

你这个问题非常好，下面按“**入参含义** → **两张表作用** → **每个字段作用** → **整体数据流**”讲清楚。

## 1) `/api/kb/ingest` 入参各字段作用

对应 DTO 是 `src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/model/dto/IngestRequest.java`，字段如下：

- `title`
  - 文档标题。
  - 用于入库到主表 `kb_source_document.title`，也会写入 chunk 的 metadata。
  - 如果你不传，代码会自动用 `sourceUrl` 或正文前缀推导一个标题。

- `sourceUrl`
  - 文档来源地址（如 PDF 链接）。
  - 当 `content` 为空时，系统会去这个 URL 抓取正文（PDF 用 PDF 解析，HTML 用 Jsoup）。
  - 会入库到 `kb_source_document.source_url`。

- `sourceOrg`
  - 来源机构（如“国家卫生健康委员会”）。
  - 作为来源标识，入库并用于后续检索结果展示。

- `docType`
  - 文档类型标签（如 `guideline`、`consensus`）。
  - 用于分类检索、过滤、统计。

- `content`
  - 原文内容（可选）。
  - **优先级高于 `sourceUrl`**：如果传了 `content`，系统直接用它，不再联网下载。
  - 适合你已离线下载好 PDF 并抽取文本后再入库。

---

## 2) 两张表各自作用（`V1__init_knowledge_tables.sql`）

在 `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__init_knowledge_tables.sql` 里：

### `kb_source_document`（文档主表）
存“一篇文档”的基础信息：标题、来源、类型、状态、去重校验等。  
一行代表一个完整文档。

### `kb_document_chunk`（切片表）
存“文档切片”信息。  
一篇文档会被切成多个 chunk，每个 chunk 一行，记录 chunk 文本、编号、token 估算、向量映射 ID 等。

---

## 3) 每个字段的含义和用途

### 表一：`kb_source_document`

- `id`：主键，自增，文档唯一 ID
- `title`：文档标题，检索回显来源时会用到
- `source_url`：来源 URL，便于追溯与跳转
- `source_org`：来源机构
- `doc_type`：文档类型标签
- `checksum`：文档内容哈希（SHA-256），用于去重
- `status`：文档状态（默认 `READY`，可扩展为 `FAILED`/`PROCESSING`）
- `published_at`：文档发布日期（当前可空，后续可补）
- `created_at` / `updated_at`：审计字段

约束：
- `uk_kb_source_document_checksum` 唯一索引：同内容不重复导入

### 表二：`kb_document_chunk`

- `id`：主键，自增，切片唯一 ID
- `document_id`：外键，关联 `kb_source_document.id`
- `chunk_no`：切片序号（1,2,3...）
- `chunk_text`：切片正文
- `token_count`：token 估算值（用于后续上下文拼接/成本控制）
- `vector_doc_id`：与向量库记录的映射 ID（关键桥接字段）
- `metadata_json`：切片元数据 JSON（来源、标题、类型等冗余信息）
- `created_at`：创建时间

约束与索引：
- 外键 `document_id` + `ON DELETE CASCADE`：删文档会自动删所有切片
- `uk_kb_document_chunk_doc_chunk(document_id, chunk_no)`：防重复切片号
- `idx_kb_document_chunk_vector_doc_id`：通过向量命中回查业务数据更快

---

## 4) 这套设计在你项目里的实际作用

结合 `src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/service/IngestionService.java`：

1. 解析入参内容（`content` 优先，否则从 `sourceUrl` 抓取）
2. 算 `checksum`，查重（避免重复导入）
3. 写入 `kb_source_document`
4. 按规则切片
5. 每个切片写入向量库（`vectorStore.add`）
6. 同时写入 `kb_document_chunk`（保存 `vector_doc_id` 映射）

这样你后续做 RAG 时：
- 先在向量库命中语义相关 chunk
- 再通过 `vector_doc_id` 回查 `kb_document_chunk` + `kb_source_document`
- 最终返回“答案 + 来源标题/URL/机构”，可追溯、可审计。

---

如果你愿意，我可以下一步给你一份“**这两个表在医疗知识库场景下建议再加的 5 个字段**”（比如 `version_no`、`effective_date`、`department_tag`、`is_current`、`source_file_name`），方便后面做指南版本管理。

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

结论先说：**你这个项目当前阶段不一定更好，反而可能增加复杂度**。

你现在的持久层是两部分：

- 向量检索：`Spring AI VectorStore (MariaDB)`（这一层本来就不是 MyBatis-Plus 管的）
- 业务表：`JdbcTemplate`（`kb_source_document`、`kb_document_chunk`）

所以“换成 MyBatis-Plus”只会替换第二部分，向量检索部分不变。

## 什么时候换 MyBatis-Plus 更好

适合换的场景：

- 业务表会快速变多（10+表），CRUD 很多
- 你希望统一分页、动态条件、逻辑删除、自动填充
- 团队已经有 MyBatis-Plus 规范和经验
- 后续会有复杂后台管理（列表筛选、统计报表）

不太适合换的场景（你现在更像）：

- 目前只有 2 张业务表
- SQL 结构简单，写法可控
- 核心难点在“抓取/切片/向量检索”，不在 ORM
- 你已经跑通链路，优先稳定

## 对你当前项目的建议

- **短期**：先不换，继续用 `JdbcTemplate`，把导入稳定性、去重、重试、来源追溯做好。
- **中期**：如果你要做管理后台和复杂查询，再把 `kb_*` 两表迁到 MyBatis-Plus。
- **长期**：即使迁移，也保持“向量层（VectorStore）+ 业务层（MyBatis-Plus）”双层架构，不要试图让 MP 接管向量检索。

有，而且你这个场景非常适合做成**批量导入任务**，不要手工一条条调接口。

你现在最实用的方案有 2 个：

## 方案一（立刻可用）：写脚本批量调用现有 `/api/kb/ingest`
你不用改后端，直接本地脚本循环 URL 列表即可。

### PowerShell 示例（你当前是 Windows）
```powershell
$api = "http://localhost:8887/api/kb/ingest"
$urls = @(
  "https://www.nhc.gov.cn/sps/c100088/202301/f01895a06c5349ef999f25da833c166d/files/1732844468193_68545.pdf",
  "https://www.nhc.gov.cn/ylyjs/gzdt/202407/256b4eb8398440a8811344c7be50a333.shtml",
  "https://hbp-office.nccd.org.cn/download.html"
  # ...其余URL
)

foreach ($u in $urls) {
  $body = @{
    title     = ""
    sourceUrl = $u
    sourceOrg = "批量导入"
    docType   = "guideline"
  } | ConvertTo-Json

  try {
    $resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri $api -ContentType "application/json" -Body $body
    Write-Host "[OK] $u => docId=$($resp.documentId), chunks=$($resp.chunkCount), duplicated=$($resp.duplicated)"
  } catch {
    Write-Host "[FAIL] $u => $($_.Exception.Message)"
  }
}
```

---

## 方案二（推荐长期）：做“批量导入接口 + 异步任务”
新增一个批量接口，比如 `/api/kb/ingest/batch`，一次提交多个 URL，后台异步处理：

- 入库任务表（待处理/处理中/成功/失败）
- 失败重试（3 次）
- 并发抓取（例如 3~5 线程）
- 导入报告（成功数、失败数、失败原因）

这样你后续维护最省力。

---

## 你这批 URL 的特别点（“HTML 或里面对应内容”）
很多 `.shtml` 页面本身不是正文，而是“目录页/通知页”，正文在页面里的附件链接里（PDF、DOC）。

所以应当用这个规则：

1. 先抓取页面正文（HTML）
2. 再提取页面里的附件链接（`href` 包含 `.pdf/.doc/.docx/.html/.shtml`）
3. 对附件链接继续入库（建议只抓同域名白名单）
4. 用你现有 `checksum` 去重，避免重复写向量

---

## 最佳实践建议（按优先级）
- 先用**方案一脚本**，今天就能跑通批量导入
- 下一步做**方案二异步批任务**
- 增加一个“仅抓一级链接”的开关（避免无限爬取）
- 给每条 URL 记录状态和错误信息，便于补采

- 自动识别 HTML 页面附件
- 去重
- 重试
- 失败日志导出 CSV。

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex)**_

你这个判断完全正确。针对你这类“**通知页 + 附件页**”站点，建议做成一个**两阶段批处理管道**，而不是单条 ingest。

下面给你一个可以直接落地到你当前项目的实现方式（基于你已有的 `IngestionService` 和 `checksum` 去重）。

---

## 实现思路（推荐）

### 阶段1：抓取页面 + 提取可跟进链接
对每个种子 URL：

1. 下载页面正文（HTML）
2. 用 `Jsoup` 提取 `a[href]`
3. 过滤出目标后缀：`.pdf/.doc/.docx/.html/.shtml`
4. 处理相对路径为绝对 URL（`absUrl("href")`）
5. 只保留白名单域名（如 `nhc.gov.cn`, `nccd.org.cn`, `gov.cn`, `cma.org.cn`）

### 阶段2：对“正文页/附件”执行入库
对阶段1产生的 URL，调用你现有 ingest 逻辑（或复用内部方法）：

- PDF/DOC/DOCX：抽取文本 -> 切片 -> 向量化 -> 写 MariaDB + VectorStore
- HTML/SHTML：抽正文 -> 入库（并可继续提取下一层附件）

---

## 你项目里建议新增的能力

### 1) 新增一个批量接口（一次提交多个 URL）
`POST /api/kb/ingest/batch`

请求示例：
```json
{
  "seedUrls": [
    "https://www.nhc.gov.cn/ylyjs/gzdt/202407/256b4eb8398440a8811344c7be50a333.shtml"
  ],
  "allowedDomains": ["nhc.gov.cn", "gov.cn", "nccd.org.cn", "cma.org.cn"],
  "maxDepth": 1,
  "maxUrls": 500,
  "followAttachmentLinks": true,
  "docType": "guideline",
  "sourceOrg": "国家卫生健康委员会"
}
```

---

## 核心算法（BFS 最稳）

```java
Queue<UrlTask> queue = new ArrayDeque<>();
Set<String> visited = new HashSet<>();

// init
seedUrls.forEach(u -> queue.add(new UrlTask(normalize(u), 0)));

while (!queue.isEmpty() && visited.size() < maxUrls) {
    UrlTask task = queue.poll();
    if (!visited.add(task.url())) continue;
    if (!isAllowedDomain(task.url(), allowedDomains)) continue;

    // 1) 入库当前 URL（复用你已有 ingest 逻辑）
    ingestUrl(task.url(), sourceOrg, docType);

    // 2) 深度控制
    if (task.depth() >= maxDepth) continue;

    // 3) 如果是 html/shtml，则提取子链接
    if (isHtmlLike(task.url())) {
        String html = fetchHtml(task.url());
        Set<String> links = extractCandidateLinks(html, task.url());
        for (String link : links) {
            if (isTargetSuffix(link) && isAllowedDomain(link, allowedDomains)) {
                queue.add(new UrlTask(normalize(link), task.depth() + 1));
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 链接提取关键实现点

### HTML 提取链接（Jsoup）
```java
Document doc = Jsoup.connect(pageUrl).userAgent("Mozilla/5.0").get();
Elements anchors = doc.select("a[href]");
for (Element a : anchors) {
    String abs = a.absUrl("href"); // 自动把相对路径转绝对
    // 过滤扩展名 + 域名
}
```

### 扩展名过滤（建议）
- 文档类：`.pdf .doc .docx`
- 页面类：`.html .shtml`
- 也可通过 `Content-Type` 兜底判断，不只靠后缀

---

## 去重策略（你现在已有一半）

你已经有 `checksum` 去重（内容级别）了，很好。建议再加两层：

1. **运行时 URL 去重**：`visited Set`
2. **库内 URL 去重（可选）**：给 `kb_source_document.source_url` 增加唯一索引（或 URL 哈希字段）

这样可以避免同一 URL 多次抓取，且内容重复也不会重复向量化。

---

## 实操参数建议（先跑稳）

- `maxDepth = 1`（先抓“通知页 -> 附件”，不要递归太深）
- `maxUrls = 300~500`
- 线程并发 `3~5`
- 每个 URL 超时 `30~60s`
- 失败重试 `2~3` 次
- 记录失败原因（握手失败、404、解析失败等）

---

## 你当前代码怎么对接

- 你已有单条入口：`/api/kb/ingest`
- 你已有内容获取与切片：`IngestionService`
- 新增 `BatchIngestionService` 最合适，不要把批量逻辑硬塞进当前 `ingest(IngestRequest)`
